meta.name: NPI-16 Narcissistic Personality Inventory
meta.description: The NPI-16 is a short form of the Narcissistic Personality Inventory, measuring subclinical narcissism using 16 forced-choice items. Each item presents two statements; one reflects narcissistic tendencies and the other does not.
meta.author: Ames, Rose & Anderson (2006)
meta.reference: Ames DR, Rose P, Anderson CP. The NPI-16 as a short measure of narcissism. J Res Pers. 2006;40(4):440-450.
meta.instruction: For each pair of statements below, choose the one that you most closely identify with. There are no right or wrong answers.

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I really like to be the center of attention
It makes me uncomfortable to be the center of attention

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I am no better or no worse than most people
I think I am a special person

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: Everybody likes to hear my stories
Sometimes I tell good stories

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I usually get the respect that I deserve
I insist upon getting the respect that is due me

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I don't mind following orders
I like having authority over people

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I am going to be a great person
I hope I am going to be successful

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: People sometimes believe what I tell them
I can make anybody believe anything I want them to

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I expect a great deal from other people
I like to do things for other people

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I like to be the center of attention
I prefer to blend in with the crowd

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I am an extraordinary person
I am much like everybody else

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I always know what I am doing
Sometimes I am not sure of what I am doing

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I don't like it when I find myself manipulating people
I find it easy to manipulate people

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I insist upon getting the respect that is due me
I usually get the respect that I deserve

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I try not to be a show off
I am apt to show off if I get the chance

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I am more capable than other people
There is a lot that I can learn from other people

questions.text: Which statement do you most identify with?

choices.label: I have a strong will to power
Power for its own sake doesn't interest me

ranges.label: Low Narcissism
ranges.description: Your score indicates a low level of narcissistic traits. You tend to be modest and may undervalue your own qualities. This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
suggestions: Your modesty is admirable, but ensure you also recognize your strengths
Practice healthy self-assertion when appropriate
Balance humility with confidence in your abilities
Understanding your personality traits supports personal growth
Every person has unique strengths and value in their personality

ranges.label: Below Average
ranges.description: Your narcissism score is below the average range. You demonstrate a healthy balance of self-regard. This indicates that this personality dimension falls within the typical range commonly seen in the general population.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a balanced perspective on yourself
Your self-awareness is a positive quality
Feel free to acknowledge your accomplishments
Understanding your personality traits supports personal growth
Every person has unique strengths and value in their personality

ranges.label: Average
ranges.description: Your narcissism score falls within the average range. Most people score in this range, reflecting typical levels of self-confidence. This level of trait expression is slightly above average but within the normal range of individual variation.
suggestions: Your level of self-regard is within the normal range
Continue to balance confidence with empathy for others
Be mindful of how your self-confidence is perceived by others
Learn how this trait influences your behavior and relationships
Leverage the positive aspects of this trait

ranges.label: Above Average
ranges.description: Your score is above average, suggesting relatively elevated narcissistic traits such as self-importance and need for admiration. This trait is notably expressed and may influence your interpersonal interactions and self-perception in certain situations.
suggestions: Practice active listening and showing genuine interest in others
Seek honest feedback from trusted people in your life
Reflect on how your behavior may affect those around you
Consider whether your expectations of others are reasonable
Gain deeper insight into how this trait affects your relationships

ranges.label: High Narcissism
ranges.description: Your score indicates a high level of narcissistic traits. This may include strong feelings of superiority, need for admiration, and sense of entitlement.
suggestions: Consider reflecting on your relationships and how others respond to you
Practice empathy by considering situations from others' perspectives
A counselor or therapist can help explore these traits in a supportive environment
Work on developing genuine reciprocity in your relationships
Consider discussing the impact of personality traits with a counselor
